content:
Case 6:
Initial presentation:
- Age: 67
- Sex: male
- Chief complaint: New painful nodular skin lesions on the left forearm with systemic symptoms
- Vital signs: BP 128/76 mmHg, HR 82/min (regular), Temp 36.8 °C, RR 16/min, SpO₂ 96% on room air.
Setting: Outpatient/Day Unit (Initial Presentation in a Post-transplant Patient)
Capabilities: You are evaluating a clinically stable but symptomatic patient in an ambulatory or day-unit setting. You can obtain a focused history and examination, request routine blood tests, and arrange standard non-invasive imaging. Invasive tissue sampling and complex diagnostics usually require further coordination and are not performed at the first contact.
Provide either a request for a single information item or solve.

{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Provide detailed transplant history (type of organ transplanted, date of transplant, current immunosuppressive medications), description of the skin lesions (onset, number, size, color, tenderness, distribution, any ulceration or discharge), specifics of systemic symptoms (fever, weight loss, fatigue, joint pains, etc.), relevant past medical history, and physical examination findings especially of the skin lesions and any lymphadenopathy or organomegaly."}}

response.request: Provide detailed transplant history (type of organ transplanted, date of transplant, current immunosuppressive medications), description of the skin lesions (onset, number, size, color, tenderness, distribution, any ulceration or discharge), specifics of systemic symptoms (fever, weight loss, fatigue, joint pains, etc.), relevant past medical history, and physical examination findings especially of the skin lesions and any lymphadenopathy or organomegaly.

content:
History Physical:
January 7, 2024: The patient presented to the clinic with new, painful nodular skin lesions on the left forearm that had developed over the previous two weeks, associated with erythema, tenderness, and occasional pruritus. He reported progressive fatigue, decreased exercise tolerance, and mild exertional dyspnea. Additional symptoms included night sweats and an unintentional weight loss of approximately 3 kg over the past two months. During the last week, the patient experienced intermittent headaches, transient blurred vision while reading, and occasional tingling around the right corner of the mouth. He denied any seizures or focal motor deficits.
Physical examination: Multiple violaceous, indurated nodules (2-2.5 cm) were noted on the left forearm, tender to palpation, with additional smaller papular lesions on the anterior chest wall. The skin was otherwise dry, without ulcerations. The conjunctivae appeared pale. No oral mucosal lesions were observed. Bilateral axillary lymphadenopathy (1-1.5 cm), hepatomegaly, and a palpable spleen were present.
Neurological examination: Mildly slowed saccadic eye movements to the right, hypoesthesia in the right infraorbital region, and subtle unsteadiness when walking heel-to-toe. Cranial nerves otherwise intact; no motor weakness or dysarthria. Lungs were clear on auscultation.
Current medication: Amlodipine 5 mg 1-0-0, bisoprolol 2.5 mg 1-0-0, atorvastatin 20 mg 0-0-1, pantoprazole 40 mg 1-0-0, escitalopram 10 mg 1-0-0, tiotropium inhaler 1-0-0, vitamin D 20,000 IU weekly, belumosudil 200mg 1-0-1, valaciclovir 500mg 1-0-0, co-trimoxazole 480 mg 1-0-0 (Mo/Wed/Fr), apixiban 2.5mg 1-0-1.

{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Routine blood tests including complete blood count (CBC) with differential, comprehensive metabolic panel (CMP) including liver function tests, LDH, CRP, ESR, quantitative EBV PCR, CMV PCR, and blood cultures."}}

response.request: Routine blood tests including complete blood count (CBC) with differential, comprehensive metabolic panel (CMP) including liver function tests, LDH, CRP, ESR, quantitative EBV PCR, CMV PCR, and blood cultures.

content:
Cbc Chemistry:
CBC: Hb 9.7 g/dL, WBC 2.7 ×10⁹/L, ANC 1.1 ×10⁹/L, Plt 83 ×10⁹/L; MCV normal, MCH normal; differential: 40% neutrophils, 22% monocytes, 35% lymphocytes, 3% nuclear debris.
Coagulation: aPTT 32 sec, INR 1.6.
Chemistry: Na 126 mmol/L, K 4.1 mmol/L, creatinine 1.6 mg/dL, BUN 23 mg/dL, AST 76 U/L, ALT 86 U/L, bilirubin 1.3 mg/dL, LDH 289 U/L, uric acid 7.2 mg/dL, Ca²⁺ 2.75 mmol/L, phosphate 3.4 mg/dL, lactate 1.3 mmol/L, CK 89 U/L, Troponin T 0.012 ng/mL, NT-proBNP 235 pg/mL.

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Post-transplant lymphoproliferative disorder (PTLD), likely EBV-driven given the immunosuppressed state (belumosudil suggests post-allogeneic HSCT with cGVHD), presenting with cutaneous involvement, bone marrow infiltration (pancytopenia), systemic B symptoms, hepatosplenomegaly, and possible CNS involvement.", "differentials": ["Disseminated fungal infection (e.g., histoplasmosis or cryptococcosis)", "Primary CNS lymphoma", "Systemic EBV-associated lymphoma outside PTLD context", "Richter transformation if underlying CLL"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Arrange urgent dermatology referral for excisional biopsy of skin lesion with histopathology, flow cytometry, EBV in situ hybridization, and fungal stains/cultures", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Quantitative plasma EBV PCR (if not already pending) and bone marrow biopsy", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "CT chest/abdomen/pelvis to evaluate lymphadenopathy and organomegaly", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "MRI brain with contrast for neurological symptoms", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Reduce immunosuppression (e.g., hold or taper belumosudil after specialist input) pending biopsy results", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Consider rituximab 375 mg/m\u00b2 weekly x4 if biopsy confirms CD20+ PTLD", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Transfuse packed red blood cells if Hb <8 g/dL or symptomatic anemia; platelets if <10k or bleeding", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Hyponatremia workup (urine osmolality/sodium) and fluid restriction if SIADH", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Post-transplant lymphoproliferative disorder (PTLD), likely EBV-driven given the immunosuppressed state (belumosudil suggests post-allogeneic HSCT with cGVHD), presenting with cutaneous involvement, bone marrow infiltration (pancytopenia), systemic B symptoms, hepatosplenomegaly, and possible CNS involvement.
differentials: Disseminated fungal infection (e.g., histoplasmosis or cryptococcosis)
Systemic EBV-associated lymphoma outside PTLD context

treatment_plan.action: Arrange urgent dermatology referral for excisional biopsy of skin lesion with histopathology, flow cytometry, EBV in situ hybridization, and fungal stains/cultures
Quantitative plasma EBV PCR (if not already pending) and bone marrow biopsy
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis to evaluate lymphadenopathy and organomegaly
MRI brain with contrast for neurological symptoms
Reduce immunosuppression (e.g., hold or taper belumosudil after specialist input) pending biopsy results
Consider rituximab 375 mg/m² weekly x4 if biopsy confirms CD20+ PTLD
Transfuse packed red blood cells if Hb <8 g/dL or symptomatic anemia; platelets if <10k or bleeding
Hyponatremia workup (urine osmolality/sodium) and fluid restriction if SIADH

Round 2 begins.
Setting: Inpatient Ward (Early Hematologic and Dermatologic Workup)
Capabilities: The patient is now admitted for further evaluation of suspected systemic disease. You can organize invasive diagnostic procedures such as marrow and tissue sampling, detailed histopathology and immunophenotyping, and cross-sectional imaging review. Interdisciplinary consultations are available. Some specialized analyses may still be pending.
Request additional information or solve.

response.request: Results of excisional biopsy of the skin lesion including histopathology, immunohistochemistry, flow cytometry, EBV in situ hybridization, and microbiology (fungal stains/cultures); bone marrow biopsy with aspirate findings, flow cytometry, cytogenetics; plasma EBV and CMV PCR quantitative; CT chest/abdomen/pelvis report; MRI brain report.

content:
Skin Biopsy:
Dermatopathology (skin biopsy, left forearm): Dense dermal infiltrate of medium-sized, atypical, monomorphic cells with irregular nuclei, fine chromatin, and scant cytoplasm, sparing the epidermis. Immunohistochemistry revealed strong positivity for CD123, CD4, CD56, and TCL1, while markers of myeloid or stem cell lineage (MPO, CD34, CD117) were negative.

{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Bone marrow biopsy and aspirate results including morphology, flow cytometry, immunohistochemistry, cytogenetics/FISH; quantitative plasma EBV PCR and CMV PCR; CT chest/abdomen/pelvis report; MRI brain with contrast report; any additional IHC on skin biopsy (e.g., CD20, CD3, TdT, TCF4)."}}

response.request: Bone marrow biopsy and aspirate results including morphology, flow cytometry, immunohistochemistry, cytogenetics/FISH; quantitative plasma EBV PCR and CMV PCR; CT chest/abdomen/pelvis report; MRI brain with contrast report; any additional IHC on skin biopsy (e.g., CD20, CD3, TdT, TCF4).

content:
Bone Marrow:
Morphology: The bone marrow was hypocellular, with markedly reduced trilineage hematopoiesis. Erythropoiesis showed nuclear atypia and was relatively decreased, with the erythroid-to-granulocytic ratio shifted in favor of the granulocytic lineage. Granulopoiesis demonstrated hypogranulated precursors with occasional abnormal mitotic figures. Megakaryopoiesis revealed increased numbers of immature forms with atypical morphology. There was infiltration by blastic cells comprising approximately 6% of total nucleated cells. The infiltrating cells were medium-sized, with irregular nuclei, fine chromatin, and scant cytoplasm.
Flow cytometry: The abnormal population was positive for CD4, TCF4, CD303, CD56, and CD123, and negative for lineage-specific B- and T-cell markers (CD3, CD14, CD19, CD34, MPO).

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Blastic plasmacytoid dendritic cell neoplasm (BPDCN)", "differentials": ["Acute myeloid leukemia with blastic infiltration", "T-lymphoblastic leukemia/lymphoma", "Peripheral T-cell lymphoma with blastic morphology"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Consult hematology/oncology and dermatology for confirmation and initiation of tagraxofusp (SL-401) therapy if CD123+ confirmed, or hyper-CVAD chemotherapy", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Complete staging with PET-CT or CT chest/abdomen/pelvis and MRI brain to assess disease extent and CNS involvement", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Lumbar puncture with CSF cytology, flow cytometry, and EBV/CMV PCR for CNS evaluation", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Transfuse PRBCs to Hb >8 g/dL, platelets to >10-20 x10^9/L; G-CSF if prolonged neutropenia", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Continue antifungal/antiviral prophylaxis; consider empiric broad-spectrum antibiotics if febrile neutropenia develops", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Taper immunosuppression cautiously after multidisciplinary discussion", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Monitor LFTs, renal function, and electrolytes; correct hyponatremia", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 2}]}}}

differentials: Acute myeloid leukemia with blastic infiltration
Peripheral T-cell lymphoma with blastic morphology

treatment_plan.action: Consult hematology/oncology and dermatology for confirmation and initiation of tagraxofusp (SL-401) therapy if CD123+ confirmed, or hyper-CVAD chemotherapy
Complete staging with PET-CT or CT chest/abdomen/pelvis and MRI brain to assess disease extent and CNS involvement
Lumbar puncture with CSF cytology, flow cytometry, and EBV/CMV PCR for CNS evaluation
Transfuse PRBCs to Hb >8 g/dL, platelets to >10-20 x10^9/L; G-CSF if prolonged neutropenia
Continue antifungal/antiviral prophylaxis; consider empiric broad-spectrum antibiotics if febrile neutropenia develops
Taper immunosuppression cautiously after multidisciplinary discussion
Monitor LFTs, renal function, and electrolytes; correct hyponatremia

Round 3 begins.
Setting: Inpatient Ward / Post-transplant Relapse Strategy Discussion
Capabilities: You now have results from specialized tests with longer turnaround times, including molecular profiling, chimerism and mutation status, and a comprehensive treatment history. You are expected to integrate these data with comorbidities and prior transplant course to define prognosis and formulate a definitive treatment and supportive care strategy.
Request additional information or solve.
Final round - provide definitive diagnosis and treatment plan.

content: {"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Blastic plasmacytoid dendritic cell neoplasm (BPDCN) in a post-allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant patient with chronic GVHD on belumosudil", "differentials": ["Donor-derived blastic neoplasm mimicking BPDCN", "Myeloid sarcoma / leukemia cutis from underlying donor cell leukemia", "Extranodal NK/T-cell lymphoma, nasal type"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Confirm chimerism studies on bone marrow (donor vs recipient engraftment in infiltrate) and molecular profiling (e.g., TET2, ASXL1 mutations) for definitive lineage assignment", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Initiate tagraxofusp (sl-401) 12 mcg/kg IV days 1-5 every 21 days if CD123 confirmed and no contraindications, or ALL-like regimen (hyper-CVAD) as bridge", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Lumbar puncture for CSF analysis, flow cytometry, cytology; intrathecal methotrexate if positive", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "PET-CT for full staging and response assessment; consider allogeneic HCT referral if CR achieved despite prior transplant", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Supportive transfusions (PRBC, platelets), G-CSF for neutropenia, antimicrobials prophylaxis (continue valacyclovir, co-trimoxazole; add azole)", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Multidisciplinary discussion (hematology, BMT, dermatology) for GVHD management - hold belumosudil if possible, transition to steroids +/- ruxolitinib if flare", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Monitor and manage hyponatremia (fluid restriction, demeclocycline if SIADH), LFTs (hold hepatotoxic meds), tumor lysis prophylaxis (allopurinol, hydration)", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 2}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Blastic plasmacytoid dendritic cell neoplasm (BPDCN) in a post-allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant patient with chronic GVHD on belumosudil
differentials: Myeloid sarcoma / leukemia cutis from underlying donor cell leukemia

treatment_plan.action: Confirm chimerism studies on bone marrow (donor vs recipient engraftment in infiltrate) and molecular profiling (e.g., TET2, ASXL1 mutations) for definitive lineage assignment
Initiate tagraxofusp (sl-401) 12 mcg/kg IV days 1-5 every 21 days if CD123 confirmed and no contraindications, or ALL-like regimen (hyper-CVAD) as bridge
Lumbar puncture for CSF analysis, flow cytometry, cytology; intrathecal methotrexate if positive
PET-CT for full staging and response assessment; consider allogeneic HCT referral if CR achieved despite prior transplant
Supportive transfusions (PRBC, platelets), G-CSF for neutropenia, antimicrobials prophylaxis (continue valacyclovir, co-trimoxazole; add azole)
Multidisciplinary discussion (hematology, BMT, dermatology) for GVHD management - hold belumosudil if possible, transition to steroids +/- ruxolitinib if flare
Monitor and manage hyponatremia (fluid restriction, demeclocycline if SIADH), LFTs (hold hepatotoxic meds), tumor lysis prophylaxis (allopurinol, hydration)
